types.name: ApertureDocument
types.description: A single control-plane document. Upserted by (kind, name). `kind` is one of Aperture's document kinds (savedView | workflowDraft | config); `name` is the document key (view name / workflowId / config key); `payload` is the JSON-string versioned envelope.

types.name: String
types.description: The `String` scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text.

types.name: ID
types.description: The `ID` scalar type represents a unique identifier, often used to refetch an object or as key for a cache. The ID type appears in a JSON response as a String; however, it is not intended to be human-readable. When expected as an input type, any string (such as `"4"`) or integer (such as `4`) input value will be accepted as an ID.

types.name: Boolean
types.description: The `Boolean` scalar type represents `true` or `false`.

types.name: Int
types.description: The `Int` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1.

types.name: DateTime
types.description: ISO-8601 datetime string (UTC).

types.name: ApertureDocumentPage
types.description: Offset-paginated ApertureDocument result set (mirrors the SDK's PaginatedResult).

types.name: FilterInput
types.description: Field/value equality filter (SDK query filter).

types.name: JSON
types.description: The `JSON` scalar type represents JSON values as specified by [ECMA-404](https://ecma-international.org/wp-content/uploads/ECMA-404_2nd_edition_december_2017.pdf).

types.name: FilterMode
types.description: How multiple filters compose (SDK filter_mode).

types.name: Author
types.description: A person who writes Books. `name` is full-text searchable; the write loop creates an Author, and the atomic-workflow loop creates an Author and its Books in one batch (intra-batch reference: the Book's `author` points at the not-yet-committed Author by its batch ref).

types.name: AuthorPage
types.description: Offset-paginated Author result set (mirrors the SDK's PaginatedResult).

types.name: Book
types.description: A published work. `format` (enum) and `in_print` (boolean) drive facets; `author` is a typed reference used for cross-links and batch resolution.

fields.name: author
fields.description: Resolved Author for `author` (graph traversal; batched per request).

types.name: BookFormat
types.description: Physical/digital form of a Book — drives an enum facet in browse/filter.

types.name: BookPage
types.description: Offset-paginated Book result set (mirrors the SDK's PaginatedResult).

types.name: ExternalID
types.description:
DEPRECATED (since 0.6.0, issue #48): use the `ExternalReference` value type on a domain slot (optionally annotated `hippo_external_xref` for reverse lookup) instead. ExternalID remains for backward compatibility only and will be removed from hippo_core in a future major release; the SDK methods (`register_external_id`, `get_by_external_id`, `list_external_ids`, mapping-level `supersede`) and the `/external-ids` REST endpoints emit DeprecationWarning / are marked deprecated in OpenAPI.
A stable cross-system identifier linking a Hippo entity to an ID managed by an external source system (e.g., STARLIMS barcode, HALO sample ID, Donor DB donor number). Lifecycle-tracked: each ExternalID instance is created, superseded, and retired via the normal entity machinery — same write path, same provenance, same query semantics as any other entity class. See sec3 §3.4 and the β-refactor handoff (sec9 PR 1.1) for context.
Note on lifecycle flags: `is_available` (inherited from Entity) is the standard entity-lifecycle flag that reflects soft-delete / supersede / archived state. `is_active` is a narrower flag meaning "this external-system mapping is currently in use". An ExternalID may be `is_available: true` (entity-level: not deleted) but `is_active: false` (mapping-level: superseded by a newer mapping).
`(source_system, value)` must be unique within active records (`is_active = true`). The uniqueness constraint is declared here as a logical `unique_keys` block; the partial-index enforcement against `is_active = true` is wired in the DDL wrapper (PR 1.2).

types.name: ExternalIDPage
types.description: Offset-paginated ExternalID result set (mirrors the SDK's PaginatedResult).

types.name: Review
types.description: A review of a Book. Third entity in the atomic-workflow batch (Author → Book → Review), so the golden path stages a three-node linked set and commits it all-or-nothing.

fields.name: book
fields.description: Resolved Book for `book` (graph traversal; batched per request).

types.name: ReviewPage
types.description: Offset-paginated Review result set (mirrors the SDK's PaginatedResult).

types.name: ProvenanceEntry
types.description: One provenance log entry for an entity (append-only audit trail; see sec6/sec9 §9.6).

types.name: SupersessionInfo
types.description: Entity-level supersession state (mirrors REST GET /entities/{id}/superseded). `supersededBy` is the direct replacement (null when the entity is current); `chain` follows replacement links forward to the terminal replacement.

types.name: XrefMatch
types.description: Entity resolved from an external reference (system, value) pair via the hippo_external_xref reverse-lookup index (issue #48). Generic envelope shape — the matching entity type is only known at query time, so `data` carries the typed payload as JSON; use `entityType` with the per-type queries for typed traversal.

types.name: HippoEntityTypeInfo
types.description: One exposed entity type from the deployment's merged LinkML schema (mirrors REST GET /schemas). This is Hippo's *domain* schema introspection — the LinkML type model the GraphQL surface itself is generated from.

types.name: HippoSlotInfo
types.description: One slot of an entity type, as classified by the shared LinkML type model (hippo.core.schema_typing).

types.name: HippoReferenceInfo
types.description: One relationship of an entity type (mirrors REST GET /schemas/{type}/references).

types.name: ApertureDocumentCreateInput
types.description: Create payload for ApertureDocument. Relationship fields take the target entity UUID.

types.name: ApertureDocumentUpdateInput
types.description: Update payload for ApertureDocument. Relationship fields take the target entity UUID.

types.name: AvailabilityResult
types.description: Result of an availability transition. Hippo never hard-deletes; lifecycle status drivers (active/archived/superseded/deleted/distributed/removed) are recorded in provenance (sec3, sec9 §9.5).

types.name: BulkAvailabilityResult
types.description: Summary of a bulk availability change (mirrors REST POST /entities/{type}/bulk-availability): per-record error isolation — failures never roll back sibling successes.

types.name: BulkAvailabilityFailure
types.description: One per-entity failure from a bulk availability change.

types.name: SupersedeResult
types.description: Result of a supersede operation.

types.name: AuthorCreateInput
types.description: Create payload for Author. Relationship fields take the target entity UUID.

types.name: AuthorUpdateInput
types.description: Update payload for Author. Relationship fields take the target entity UUID.

types.name: BookCreateInput
types.description: Create payload for Book. Relationship fields take the target entity UUID.

types.name: BookUpdateInput
types.description: Update payload for Book. Relationship fields take the target entity UUID.

types.name: ExternalIDCreateInput
types.description: Create payload for ExternalID. Relationship fields take the target entity UUID.

types.name: ExternalIDUpdateInput
types.description: Update payload for ExternalID. Relationship fields take the target entity UUID.

types.name: ReviewCreateInput
types.description: Create payload for Review. Relationship fields take the target entity UUID.

types.name: ReviewUpdateInput
types.description: Update payload for Review. Relationship fields take the target entity UUID.

types.name: BatchWriteGraphQLResult
types.description: Result of an atomic multi-entity write (HippoClient.batch_put). The set commits all-or-nothing; `entities`/`relationships` carry the written (or, on a dry run, planned) payloads as JSON.

types.name: BatchValidationGraphQLResult
types.description: Whole-set dry-run validation result (HippoClient.validate_batch); aggregated per-entity, no writes.

types.name: BatchEntityValidation
types.description: Per-entity validation outcome within a batch.

types.name: ValidationFailureType
types.description: One tier-annotated validation failure (sec9 §9.9).

types.name: BatchEntityInput
types.description: One entity in a batch validate/write request.

types.name: BatchRelationshipInput
types.description: One intra-batch relationship edge, created after the entities within the same atomic transaction. Source/target may reference entities created earlier in the same batch.

types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server supports subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name, description and optional `specifiedByURL`, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.
